revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
1 revision imported: importing programmatically created pages and categories

revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:To/doc]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:To/doc]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]]
#redirect[[:en:Template:To/doc]] [[sn::Automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[sn::bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:To/doc]][[sn::bump February 13, 2025 bump]]

{{documentation subpage}} {{hatnote|All "ping" and "to" templates require a '''signature with timestamp''', or the recipients '''will not be notified'''. ~~~~ (four tildes)}} {{High-use}} == Usage == This template takes advantage of the user mention [[Wikipedia:Notifications|notification]] to ping the user(s) you are replying to in a discussion (so you don't have to use the {{tl|talkback}} template). It works on any talk page or Wikipedia namespace page. This template is particularly helpful in discussions that are not threaded, to indicate which user's comment is referenced. If you feel that this template unnecessarily personalizes the discussion, you may still use the {{tl|talkback}} template on the user's talk page to indicate that you have replied to a comment. ''Note that it isn't necessary to use this template to ping (indicate) the user if you are already linking to their username from your post.'' === Single recipient === To use it, just add something like the following to an existing discussion: {{to|User1}} Message text. ~~~~ For example, ":{{to|Jimbo Wales}} Message text." displays as: :{{to|Jimbo Wales}} Message text. The comment must be signed and belong to a section in order for the notification to work. === Multiple recipients === You can reply to up to 7 people at once: {{to|User1|UserII|UserC}} Message text. ~~~~ === Specify a nickname === You can display any text in place of a username by using {{para|labelX}} or {{para|sX}}, where X is the index of a positional parameter, counting from 1. For example: * {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar|Example2|label2=John}} produces {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar|Example2|label2=John}} * {{to|Example|s1=Examp|Example2|s2=Jack}} produces {{to|Example|s1=Examp|Example2|s2=Jack}} :'''{{smallcaps|Tip!}}''' – by default, the very first letter after the {{para|labelx}} parameter is in uppercase, as in {{para|label1|exemplar}}, which produces {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar}}. A trick that uses one [[soft hyphen]], between the equal sign and the first letter, as in {{para|label1|­exemplar}} will make the first letter lowercase if needed, as in {{to|Example|label1=­exemplar}}. The soft hyphen is invisible and yet it can be copied and pasted. So here it is for you to copy and paste: =­e (between the equal sign and letter "e"). It's easiest to copy the "=­e" in the previous sentence, paste it where you want the soft hyphen to go, and then carefully erase just the "e" and the equal sign separately, which will leave the soft hyphen behind. === Customize punctuation === By default, the template will insert a colon after the final name. To change this, specify the required character using the {{para|p}} parameter. For example: * {{to|Example|p=---}} produces {{to|Example|p=---}} * {{to|Example|p=}} produces {{to|Example|p=}} == Related templates == * {{tl|reply to}} – Uses an "@" sign at the beginning instead of "To " * {{tl|user link}} – Links to a single user's page (without the "To" or the @ sign, and without a colon) * {{tl|no ping}} – Links a user's name without triggering the echo notification system {{Inline talk templates}} {{Sandbox other||{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|testcases|| [[Category:Inline talk templates]] [[Category:Username internal link templates]] }}}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{hatnote|All "ping" and "to" templates require a '''signature with timestamp''', or the recipients '''will not be notified'''. ~~~~ (four tildes)}} {{High-use}} == Usage == This template takes advantage of the user mention [[Wikipedia:Notifications|notification]] to ping the user(s) you are replying to in a discussion (so you don't have to use the {{tl|talkback}} template). It works on any talk page or Wikipedia namespace page. This template is particularly helpful in discussions that are not threaded, to indicate which user's comment is referenced. If you feel that this template unnecessarily personalizes the discussion, you may still use the {{tl|talkback}} template on the user's talk page to indicate that you have replied to a comment. ''Note that it isn't necessary to use this template to ping (indicate) the user if you are already linking to their username from your post.'' === Single recipient === To use it, just add something like the following to an existing discussion: {{to|User1}} Message text. ~~~~ For example, ":{{to|Jimbo Wales}} Message text." displays as: :{{to|Jimbo Wales}} Message text. The comment must be signed and belong to a section in order for the notification to work. === Multiple recipients === You can reply to up to 7 people at once: {{to|User1|UserII|UserC}} Message text. ~~~~ === Specify a nickname === You can display any text in place of a username by using {{para|labelX}} or {{para|sX}}, where X is the index of a positional parameter, counting from 1. For example: * {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar|Example2|label2=John}} produces {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar|Example2|label2=John}} * {{to|Example|s1=Examp|Example2|s2=Jack}} produces {{to|Example|s1=Examp|Example2|s2=Jack}} :'''{{smallcaps|Tip!}}''' – by default, the very first letter after the {{para|labelx}} parameter is in uppercase, as in {{para|label1|exemplar}}, which produces {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar}}. A trick that uses one [[:en:Soft hyphen|Soft hyphen]], between the equal sign and the first letter, as in {{para|label1|­exemplar}} will make the first letter lowercase if needed, as in {{to|Example|label1=­exemplar}}. The soft hyphen is invisible and yet it can be copied and pasted. So here it is for you to copy and paste: =­e (between the equal sign and letter "e"). It's easiest to copy the "=­e" in the previous sentence, paste it where you want the soft hyphen to go, and then carefully erase just the "e" and the equal sign separately, which will leave the soft hyphen behind. === Customize punctuation === By default, the template will insert a colon after the final name. To change this, specify the required character using the {{para|p}} parameter. For example: * {{to|Example|p=---}} produces {{to|Example|p=---}} * {{to|Example|p=}} produces {{to|Example|p=}} == Related templates == * {{tl|reply to}} – Uses an "@" sign at the beginning instead of "To " * {{tl|user link}} – Links to a single user's page (without the "To" or the @ sign, and without a colon) * {{tl|no ping}} – Links a user's name without triggering the echo notification system {{Inline talk templates}} {{Sandbox other||{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|testcases|| [[Category:Inline talk templates]] [[Category:Username internal link templates]] }}}}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{hatnote|All "ping" and "to" templates require a '''signature with timestamp''', or the recipients '''will not be notified'''. ~~~~ (four tildes)}} {{High-use}} == Usage == This template takes advantage of the user mention [[Wikipedia:Notifications|notification]] to ping the user(s) you are replying to in a discussion (so you don't have to use the {{tl|talkback}} template). It works on any talk page or Wikipedia namespace page. This template is particularly helpful in discussions that are not threaded, to indicate which user's comment is referenced. If you feel that this template unnecessarily personalizes the discussion, you may still use the {{tl|talkback}} template on the user's talk page to indicate that you have replied to a comment. ''Note that it isn't necessary to use this template to ping (indicate) the user if you are already linking to their username from your post.'' === Single recipient === To use it, just add something like the following to an existing discussion: {{to|User1}} Message text. ~~~~ For example, ":{{to|Jimbo Wales}} Message text." displays as: :{{to|Jimbo Wales}} Message text. The comment must be signed and belong to a section in order for the notification to work. === Multiple recipients === You can reply to up to 7 people at once: {{to|User1|UserII|UserC}} Message text. ~~~~ === Specify a nickname === You can display any text in place of a username by using {{para|labelX}} or {{para|sX}}, where X is the index of a positional parameter, counting from 1. For example: * {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar|Example2|label2=John}} produces {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar|Example2|label2=John}} * {{to|Example|s1=Examp|Example2|s2=Jack}} produces {{to|Example|s1=Examp|Example2|s2=Jack}} :'''{{smallcaps|Tip!}}''' – by default, the very first letter after the {{para|labelx}} parameter is in uppercase, as in {{para|label1|exemplar}}, which produces {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar}}. A trick that uses one [[:en:Soft hyphen|Soft hyphen]], between the equal sign and the first letter, as in {{para|label1|­exemplar}} will make the first letter lowercase if needed, as in {{to|Example|label1=­exemplar}}. The soft hyphen is invisible and yet it can be copied and pasted. So here it is for you to copy and paste: =­e (between the equal sign and letter "e"). It's easiest to copy the "=­e" in the previous sentence, paste it where you want the soft hyphen to go, and then carefully erase just the "e" and the equal sign separately, which will leave the soft hyphen behind. === Customize punctuation === By default, the template will insert a colon after the final name. To change this, specify the required character using the {{para|p}} parameter. For example: * {{to|Example|p=---}} produces {{to|Example|p=---}} * {{to|Example|p=}} produces {{to|Example|p=}} == Related templates == * {{tl|reply to}} – Uses an "@" sign at the beginning instead of "To " * {{tl|user link}} – Links to a single user's page (without the "To" or the @ sign, and without a colon) * {{tl|no ping}} – Links a user's name without triggering the echo notification system {{Inline talk templates}} {{Sandbox other||{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|testcases|| [[Category:Inline talk templates]] [[Category:Username internal link templates]] }}}} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{hatnote|All "ping" and "to" templates require a '''signature with timestamp''', or the recipients '''will not be notified'''. ~~~~ (four tildes)}} {{High-use}} == Usage == This template takes advantage of the user mention [[Wikipedia:Notifications|notification]] to ping the user(s) you are replying to in a discussion (so you don't have to use the {{tl|talkback}} template). It works on any talk page or Wikipedia namespace page. This template is particularly helpful in discussions that are not threaded, to indicate which user's comment is referenced. If you feel that this template unnecessarily personalizes the discussion, you may still use the {{tl|talkback}} template on the user's talk page to indicate that you have replied to a comment. ''Note that it isn't necessary to use this template to ping (indicate) the user if you are already linking to their username from your post.'' === Single recipient === To use it, just add something like the following to an existing discussion: {{to|User1}} Message text. ~~~~ For example, ":{{to|Jimbo Wales}} Message text." displays as: :{{to|Jimbo Wales}} Message text. The comment must be signed and belong to a section in order for the notification to work. === Multiple recipients === You can reply to up to 7 people at once: {{to|User1|UserII|UserC}} Message text. ~~~~ === Specify a nickname === You can display any text in place of a username by using {{para|labelX}} or {{para|sX}}, where X is the index of a positional parameter, counting from 1. For example: * {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar|Example2|label2=John}} produces {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar|Example2|label2=John}} * {{to|Example|s1=Examp|Example2|s2=Jack}} produces {{to|Example|s1=Examp|Example2|s2=Jack}} :'''{{smallcaps|Tip!}}''' – by default, the very first letter after the {{para|labelx}} parameter is in uppercase, as in {{para|label1|exemplar}}, which produces {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar}}. A trick that uses one [[:en:Soft hyphen|Soft hyphen]], between the equal sign and the first letter, as in {{para|label1|­exemplar}} will make the first letter lowercase if needed, as in {{to|Example|label1=­exemplar}}. The soft hyphen is invisible and yet it can be copied and pasted. So here it is for you to copy and paste: =­e (between the equal sign and letter "e"). It's easiest to copy the "=­e" in the previous sentence, paste it where you want the soft hyphen to go, and then carefully erase just the "e" and the equal sign separately, which will leave the soft hyphen behind. === Customize punctuation === By default, the template will insert a colon after the final name. To change this, specify the required character using the {{para|p}} parameter. For example: * {{to|Example|p=---}} produces {{to|Example|p=---}} * {{to|Example|p=}} produces {{to|Example|p=}} == Related templates == * {{tl|reply to}} – Uses an "@" sign at the beginning instead of "To " * {{tl|user link}} – Links to a single user's page (without the "To" or the @ sign, and without a colon) * {{tl|no ping}} – Links a user's name without triggering the echo notification system {{Inline talk templates}} {{Sandbox other||{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|testcases|| [[Category:Inline talk templates]] [[Category:Username internal link templates]] }}}}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{hatnote|All "ping" and "to" templates require a '''signature with timestamp''', or the recipients '''will not be notified'''. ~~~~ (four tildes)}} {{High-use}} == Usage == This template takes advantage of the user mention [[Wikipedia:Notifications|notification]] to ping the user(s) you are replying to in a discussion (so you don't have to use the {{tl|talkback}} template). It works on any talk page or Wikipedia namespace page. This template is particularly helpful in discussions that are not threaded, to indicate which user's comment is referenced. If you feel that this template unnecessarily personalizes the discussion, you may still use the {{tl|talkback}} template on the user's talk page to indicate that you have replied to a comment. ''Note that it isn't necessary to use this template to ping (indicate) the user if you are already linking to their username from your post.'' === Single recipient === To use it, just add something like the following to an existing discussion: {{to|User1}} Message text. ~~~~ For example, ":{{to|Jimbo Wales}} Message text." displays as: :{{to|Jimbo Wales}} Message text. The comment must be signed and belong to a section in order for the notification to work. === Multiple recipients === You can reply to up to 7 people at once: {{to|User1|UserII|UserC}} Message text. ~~~~ === Specify a nickname === You can display any text in place of a username by using {{para|labelX}} or {{para|sX}}, where X is the index of a positional parameter, counting from 1. For example: * {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar|Example2|label2=John}} produces {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar|Example2|label2=John}} * {{to|Example|s1=Examp|Example2|s2=Jack}} produces {{to|Example|s1=Examp|Example2|s2=Jack}} :'''{{smallcaps|Tip!}}''' – by default, the very first letter after the {{para|labelx}} parameter is in uppercase, as in {{para|label1|exemplar}}, which produces {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar}}. A trick that uses one [[:en:Soft hyphen|Soft hyphen]], between the equal sign and the first letter, as in {{para|label1|­exemplar}} will make the first letter lowercase if needed, as in {{to|Example|label1=­exemplar}}. The soft hyphen is invisible and yet it can be copied and pasted. So here it is for you to copy and paste: =­e (between the equal sign and letter "e"). It's easiest to copy the "=­e" in the previous sentence, paste it where you want the soft hyphen to go, and then carefully erase just the "e" and the equal sign separately, which will leave the soft hyphen behind. === Customize punctuation === By default, the template will insert a colon after the final name. To change this, specify the required character using the {{para|p}} parameter. For example: * {{to|Example|p=---}} produces {{to|Example|p=---}} * {{to|Example|p=}} produces {{to|Example|p=}} == Related templates == * {{tl|reply to}} – Uses an "@" sign at the beginning instead of "To " * {{tl|user link}} – Links to a single user's page (without the "To" or the @ sign, and without a colon) * {{tl|no ping}} – Links a user's name without triggering the echo notification system {{Inline talk templates}} {{Sandbox other||{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|testcases|| [[Category:Inline talk templates]] [[Category:Username internal link templates]] }}}} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23630749988|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{hatnote|All "ping" and "to" templates require a '''signature with timestamp''', or the recipients '''will not be notified'''. ~~~~ (four tildes)}} {{High-use}} == Usage == This template takes advantage of the user mention [[Wikipedia:Notifications|notification]] to ping the user(s) you are replying to in a discussion (so you don't have to use the {{tl|talkback}} template). It works on any talk page or Wikipedia namespace page. This template is particularly helpful in discussions that are not threaded, to indicate which user's comment is referenced. If you feel that this template unnecessarily personalizes the discussion, you may still use the {{tl|talkback}} template on the user's talk page to indicate that you have replied to a comment. ''Note that it isn't necessary to use this template to ping (indicate) the user if you are already linking to their username from your post.'' === Single recipient === To use it, just add something like the following to an existing discussion: {{to|User1}} Message text. ~~~~ For example, ":{{to|Jimbo Wales}} Message text." displays as: :{{to|Jimbo Wales}} Message text. The comment must be signed and belong to a section in order for the notification to work. === Multiple recipients === You can reply to up to 7 people at once: {{to|User1|UserII|UserC}} Message text. ~~~~ === Specify a nickname === You can display any text in place of a username by using {{para|labelX}} or {{para|sX}}, where X is the index of a positional parameter, counting from 1. For example: * {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar|Example2|label2=John}} produces {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar|Example2|label2=John}} * {{to|Example|s1=Examp|Example2|s2=Jack}} produces {{to|Example|s1=Examp|Example2|s2=Jack}} :'''{{smallcaps|Tip!}}''' – by default, the very first letter after the {{para|labelx}} parameter is in uppercase, as in {{para|label1|exemplar}}, which produces {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar}}. A trick that uses one [[:en:Soft hyphen|Soft hyphen]], between the equal sign and the first letter, as in {{para|label1|­exemplar}} will make the first letter lowercase if needed, as in {{to|Example|label1=­exemplar}}. The soft hyphen is invisible and yet it can be copied and pasted. So here it is for you to copy and paste: =­e (between the equal sign and letter "e"). It's easiest to copy the "=­e" in the previous sentence, paste it where you want the soft hyphen to go, and then carefully erase just the "e" and the equal sign separately, which will leave the soft hyphen behind. === Customize punctuation === By default, the template will insert a colon after the final name. To change this, specify the required character using the {{para|p}} parameter. For example: * {{to|Example|p=---}} produces {{to|Example|p=---}} * {{to|Example|p=}} produces {{to|Example|p=}} == Related templates == * {{tl|reply to}} – Uses an "@" sign at the beginning instead of "To " * {{tl|user link}} – Links to a single user's page (without the "To" or the @ sign, and without a colon) * {{tl|no ping}} – Links a user's name without triggering the echo notification system {{Inline talk templates}} {{Sandbox other||{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|testcases|| }}}} [[Category:Inline talk templates]] [[Category:Username internal link templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24984760715|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{hatnote|All "ping" and "to" templates require a '''signature with timestamp''', or the recipients '''will not be notified'''. ~~~~ (four tildes)}} {{High-use}} == Usage == This template takes advantage of the user mention [[Wikipedia:Notifications|notification]] to ping the user(s) you are replying to in a discussion (so you don't have to use the {{tl|talkback}} template). It works on any talk page or Wikipedia namespace page. This template is particularly helpful in discussions that are not threaded, to indicate which user's comment is referenced. If you feel that this template unnecessarily personalizes the discussion, you may still use the {{tl|talkback}} template on the user's talk page to indicate that you have replied to a comment. ''Note that it isn't necessary to use this template to ping (indicate) the user if you are already linking to their username from your post.'' === Single recipient === To use it, just add something like the following to an existing discussion: {{to|User1}} Message text. ~~~~ For example, ":{{to|Jimbo Wales}} Message text." displays as: :{{to|Jimbo Wales}} Message text. The comment must be signed and belong to a section in order for the notification to work. === Multiple recipients === You can reply to up to 7 people at once: {{to|User1|UserII|UserC}} Message text. ~~~~ === Specify a nickname === You can display any text in place of a username by using {{para|labelX}} or {{para|sX}}, where X is the index of a positional parameter, counting from 1. For example: * {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar|Example2|label2=John}} produces {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar|Example2|label2=John}} * {{to|Example|s1=Examp|Example2|s2=Jack}} produces {{to|Example|s1=Examp|Example2|s2=Jack}} :'''{{smallcaps|Tip!}}''' – by default, the very first letter after the {{para|labelx}} parameter is in uppercase, as in {{para|label1|exemplar}}, which produces {{to|Example|label1=Exemplar}}. A trick that uses one [[:en:Soft hyphen|Soft hyphen]], between the equal sign and the first letter, as in {{para|label1|­exemplar}} will make the first letter lowercase if needed, as in {{to|Example|label1=­exemplar}}. The soft hyphen is invisible and yet it can be copied and pasted. So here it is for you to copy and paste: =­e (between the equal sign and letter "e"). It's easiest to copy the "=­e" in the previous sentence, paste it where you want the soft hyphen to go, and then carefully erase just the "e" and the equal sign separately, which will leave the soft hyphen behind. === Customize punctuation === By default, the template will insert a colon after the final name. To change this, specify the required character using the {{para|p}} parameter. For example: * {{to|Example|p=---}} produces {{to|Example|p=---}} * {{to|Example|p=}} produces {{to|Example|p=}} == Related templates == * {{tl|reply to}} – Uses an "@" sign at the beginning instead of "To " * {{tl|user link}} – Links to a single user's page (without the "To" or the @ sign, and without a colon) * {{tl|no ping}} – Links a user's name without triggering the echo notification system {{Inline talk templates}} {{Sandbox other||{{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|testcases|| }}}} [[Category:Inline talk templates]] [[Category:Username internal link templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
